prototypes.label: GLTF+AnimationMixer+IBL (H-1) + Factory+Registry (H-3) + DragControls Proxy Pattern (H-5)
prototypes.researchMethod: Reference implementation curation from official docs + authoritative tutorials

criticalGapsAddressed.canonicalDescription: MeshSSSNodeMaterial FPS cost on Chromebook (Intel Iris Xe) — benchmark required
criticalGapsAddressed.status: OPEN — research-only phase cannot resolve; runtime benchmark required
criticalGapsAddressed.researchFinding: MeshSSSNodeMaterial is a WebGPU-tier node material with additional fragment shader ALU cost. Per world-model PerformanceBudgetPolicy (obj-pr-13): SSS enable/disable is conditional on g-pr-01 prototype result. Research confirms SSS is selectively applicable (spacesuit-fabric areas only) — but Chromebook Intel Iris Xe FPS delta is unquantified without runtime stats.js benchmark.
criticalGapsAddressed.remainingGap: Must run stats.js FPS measurement on Chromebook-class GPU with SSS material active on 1 SpacekidEntity vs baseline. Target: confirm > 50fps with SSS before enabling in production.
criticalGapsAddressed.recommendedAction: T7 runtime prototype: add MeshSSSNodeMaterial to helmet-visor area, measure FPS with stats.js, compare to baseline MeshPhysicalMaterial on Chromebook dev hardware or equivalent (Intel UHD 620 / Iris Xe).
criticalGapsAddressed.ontologyObject: PerformanceBudgetPolicy (obj-pr-13) — gapDependency: conditional SSS rule blocked on this result

criticalGapsAddressed.canonicalDescription: SkeletonUtils.clone() x5 simultaneous draw call budget — Chromebook 60fps target
criticalGapsAddressed.status: OPEN — research-only phase cannot resolve; runtime draw call measurement required
criticalGapsAddressed.researchFinding: SkeletonUtils.clone() produces independent SkinnedMesh per character with separate bone matrices. Each clone = separate draw call per mesh part (typically 3-6 draw calls per character). Five spacekid instances = ~15-30 draw calls from characters alone. Three.js r183 BatchedMesh does NOT support SkinnedMesh/skeletal animation (confirmed: InstancedMesh incompatible with SkinnedMesh per clm-pr-23). No benchmarks for SkeletonUtils.clone() x5 on Chromebook Intel Iris Xe available in research sources.
criticalGapsAddressed.remainingGap: Must measure renderer.info.render.calls with 5 SkeletonUtils.clone() spacekid instances active + all AnimationMixers running. Target: stay < 100 total draw calls per PerformanceBudgetPolicy.
criticalGapsAddressed.recommendedAction: T7 runtime prototype: spawn 5 SkeletonUtils.clone() instances, call renderer.info.render.calls each frame, measure FPS with stats.js. If > 100 draw calls, evaluate LOD (Three.js LOD class) or static geometry reduction.
criticalGapsAddressed.ontologyObject: SpacekidEntityDef (obj-pr-10) forwardPropPath gap: instancing approach for SkinnedMesh unconfirmed

criticalGapsAddressed.canonicalDescription: DragControls proxy pattern + SkinnedMesh AnimationMixer concurrent — position jitter risk
criticalGapsAddressed.status: PARTIALLY_ADDRESSED — proxy pattern mechanism confirmed by reference; jitter risk remains untested
criticalGapsAddressed.researchFinding: Proxy pattern mechanism confirmed via sbcode.net/threejs/gltf-animations-drag/ — invisible BoxGeometry proxy receives DragControls events, modelGroup.position.copy(proxy.position) each frame syncs animated model. AnimationMixer.update() called independently in render loop — bone pose updates are decoupled from position copy. Theoretical jitter risk: if position.copy() fires mid-bone-update, visual pop possible. Reference implementation shows smooth operation in practice.
criticalGapsAddressed.remainingGap: No reference implementation tests crossFadeTo() (active animation blend) simultaneously with DragControls drag. The position.copy() per-frame sync may introduce 1-frame lag visible as micro-jitter during fast animation transitions. Requires runtime observation.
criticalGapsAddressed.recommendedAction: T7 runtime prototype: trigger crossFadeTo(walkAction, 0.3) while dragControls drag is active. Observe for visual jitter in character root position. If jitter observed: move position.copy() to AnimationMixer.addEventListener('loop') callback instead of requestAnimationFrame.
criticalGapsAddressed.ontologyObject: CharacterDragPlacement (ACTION) + SpatialInteractionResolver (LOGIC) — per simulator ontology object mapping

assetPipelineNote.topic: CC0 animated character asset source (previously mislabeled as g-pr-01 in prior session)
assetPipelineNote.finding: Kenney Space Kit confirmed for SpaceshipEntity (CC0 GLTF ships/planets). SpacekidEntity animated character: primary=Mixamo pipeline (FBX→Blender→GLB with embedded clips), secondary=kenney-assets.itch.io/animated-characters-3 (GLTF, requires runtime AnimationMixer compat verification). This is an asset pipeline prerequisite, not a research gap in the canonical registry.

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: const gltf = await loader.loadAsync('./spacekid.glb'); const model = gltf.scene; const clips = gltf.animations; scene.add(model);
researchFindings.integrationPoint: Replaces create-pulse-shard.ts factory function. Returns gltf.scene (Group) and gltf.animations (AnimationClip[]).
researchFindings.notes: r183 fix: 'empty groups with multiple scene references' resolved. DRACOLoader optional — use only if GLB > 500KB. import path: 'three/addons/loaders/GLTFLoader.js'

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: const mixer = new THREE.AnimationMixer(model); const idleClip = THREE.AnimationClip.findByName(clips, 'idle'); const idleAction = mixer.clipAction(idleClip); idleAction.play(); // Transition: idleAction.crossFadeTo(walkAction, 0.3, true); // In render loop: mixer.update(clock.getDelta());
researchFindings.integrationPoint: AnimationStateMachine LOGIC entity — one AnimationMixer per SkeletonUtils.clone() instance. Shared clock.getDelta() across all mixers.
researchFindings.notes: crossFadeTo(targetAction, durationSeconds, warpBoolean): both actions must be playing simultaneously for smooth blend. Reset target action before crossFade: targetAction.reset(); targetAction.play(); action.crossFadeTo(targetAction, 0.3).

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: renderer.toneMapping = THREE.ACESFilmicToneMapping; renderer.toneMappingExposure = 1.8; // renderer.outputColorSpace = THREE.SRGBColorSpace (default in r152+, no explicit set needed) const pmremGenerator = new THREE.PMREMGenerator(renderer); pmremGenerator.compileEquirectangularShader(); new RGBELoader().load('env.hdr', (texture) => { scene.environment = pmremGenerator.fromEquirectangular(texture).texture; texture.dispose(); pmremGenerator.dispose(); });
researchFindings.integrationPoint: PhotonicRealismPipeline LOGIC entity — one-time call at scene init. Auto-applies to ALL MeshStandard/PhysicalMaterial in scene. Compatible with existing n8ao + postprocessing pipeline (no OverrideMaterialManager needed).
researchFindings.notes: Poly Haven (polyhaven.com/hdris) — CC0 HDRIs 1K-8K. 1K-2K sufficient for education apps (1-8MB). scene.environment applies globally; per-material envMap property available for selective override.

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: // Spacesuit body: const suitMaterial = new THREE.MeshPhysicalMaterial({ metalness: 0.1, roughness: 0.4, color: 0xffffff, envMapIntensity: 1.0 }); // Visor: const visorMaterial = new THREE.MeshPhysicalMaterial({ metalness: 0.0, roughness: 0.0, transmission: 0.9, ior: 1.5, clearcoat: 1.0, iridescence: 0.3, iridescenceThicknessRange: [100, 400], color: 0x88ccff }); // Spaceship hull: const hullMaterial = new THREE.MeshPhysicalMaterial({ metalness: 0.8, roughness: 0.3, clearcoat: 0.5 });
researchFindings.integrationPoint: PhotonicMaterialPreset DATA entity — extends existing materials.ts. gltf.scene.traverse to apply material overrides post-load.
researchFindings.notes: Performance budget: enable only needed features per entity type. transmission most expensive. For Chromebook: visor transmission=selective, clearcoat+iridescence=cheap. MeshSSSNodeMaterial (face subsurface scattering): selective forehead/cheek areas only.

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: // Setup proxy: const proxy = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.BoxGeometry(0.5, 1.3, 0.5), new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ transparent: true, opacity: 0 })); proxy.geometry.translate(0, 0.65, 0); scene.add(proxy); // DragControls on proxy array: const dragControls = new DragControls([proxy], camera, renderer.domElement); dragControls.addEventListener('dragstart', () => { orbitControls.enabled = false; }); dragControls.addEventListener('drag', (e) => { e.object.position.y = 0; // lock to ground plane e.object.position.x = Math.round(e.object.position.x / CELL_SIZE) * CELL_SIZE; // snap grid e.object.position.z = Math.round(e.object.position.z / CELL_SIZE) * CELL_SIZE; }); dragControls.addEventListener('dragend', () => { orbitControls.enabled = true; onSpacekidPlaced(proxy.position.clone()); // trigger InteractionMode }); // Sync in render loop: modelGroup.position.copy(proxy.position);
researchFindings.integrationPoint: DragDispatch ACTION entity — integrates with interaction-controller.ts registerMode() pattern. dragend fires InteractionMode state transition.
researchFindings.notes: CRITICAL: GLTF animated models do NOT work directly with DragControls — proxy pattern is required. AnimationMixer continues uninterrupted during drag. OrbitControls must be disabled during drag to prevent camera conflict. touch-action:none on renderer.domElement prevents scroll conflict on tablet.

researchFindings.integrationPoint: Asset pipeline feeding GLTFLoader. CC0 license required for education product.
researchFindings.notes: SpaceshipEntity: Kenney Space Kit (kenney.nl/assets/space-kit) — 150 CC0 GLTF/GLB assets (ships, planets). Download, verify GLB format, load with GLTFLoader. SpacekidEntity: (1) kenney-assets.itch.io/animated-characters-3 — CC0, 4 skins + 3 animations, GLTF format (must verify AnimationMixer compat). (2) Mixamo fallback: select character → apply idle/walk/board animations → FBX with skin → Blender import → export GLB with embedded clips. (3) Sketchfab CC license filtered: sketchfab.com/tags/character (verify CC0/CC-BY license per asset).

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: import { SkeletonUtils } from 'three/addons/utils/SkeletonUtils.js'; const clonedSpacekid = SkeletonUtils.clone(baseGltf.scene); const clonedMixer = new THREE.AnimationMixer(clonedSpacekid); // Each clone has independent bone poses and AnimationMixer
researchFindings.integrationPoint: CharacterSpawn ACTION — spawns multiple independent spacekid instances from single loaded GLTF base. Each instance gets own proxy mesh for DragControls.
researchFindings.notes: SkeletonUtils.clone() produces deep clone with independent bone hierarchy. AVOID SkeletonUtils.retarget() — community reports inverted feet/backward hands with different-proportion skeletons (clm-pr-15). Embed animations in GLB file instead of runtime retargeting. Budget: ~10-25 independent SkinnedMesh instances at 60fps on Chromebook (separate AnimationMixer per instance, shared clock.getDelta()).

researchFindings.integrationPoint: PhotonicComponentFactory LOGIC entity — single dispatch point for all entity types. Pairs with H-1 GLTF stack: createSpacekidEntity fn uses GLTFLoader + AnimationMixer + SkeletonUtils.clone(). OCP: new entity type = one registry.set() call.
researchFindings.notes: Zero external dependencies — TypeScript Map<ComponentType, CreateFn>. Migration path to miniplex (H-4) is clean: replace Map<> with World, system functions become miniplex queries. Entity count target: <50 entities in mathcrew scope. H-3 preferred over H-4 for current scale per clm-int-02 OCP reasoning.

mathcrewFiles.create-pulse-shard.ts: REPLACE with create-spacekid-entity.ts — GLTFLoader + SkeletonUtils.clone() + AnimationMixer + proxy BoxGeometry. Preserve InteractionMode interface contract.
mathcrewFiles.create-resonance-loop.ts: REPLACE with create-spaceship-entity.ts — GLTFLoader (Kenney Space Kit GLB) or procedural geometry. Preserve teaching surface grouping semantics.
mathcrewFiles.materials.ts: EXTEND with photonicMaterialPresets: suitBody, visor, spaceship hull configs using MeshPhysicalMaterial. Preserve existing holographic/glow materials for non-photonic entities.
mathcrewFiles.interaction-controller.ts: ADD DragControls integration: registerDraggableEntity(spacekidProxy, onDragEnd). dragend fires existing InteractionMode state transitions. Preserve registerMode() pattern.
mathcrewFiles.teaching-story-framework.ts: METAPHOR LAYER ONLY — rename entity terms (resonance-loop→spaceship, pulse-shard→spacekid). VT/CRA/beats/ts-fsrs contracts unchanged.

src/scene/systems/animation-system.ts — per-frame mixer.update(delta) across all active AnimationMixers
src/scene/systems/drag-system.ts — DragControls setup + OrbitControls integration + snap grid logic
preservedContracts: VT/CRA/beats/ts-fsrs: unchanged — metaphor layer migration only
10 InteractionModes: registerMode() pattern preserved — dragend integrates as new ACTION trigger
n8ao + postprocessing pipeline: compatible (no OverrideMaterialManager workaround needed for MeshPhysicalMaterial)
Vector3.project() path for SpeechBubble CSS overlay: unchanged

claimsValidated: DragControls proxy pattern (invisible BoxGeometry) works with animated GLTF models — AnimationMixer unaffected (g-pr-04 RESOLVED)
PMREMGenerator + scene.environment one-time setup at init — auto-applies to all MeshPhysical/Standard (clm-pr-11)
ACESFilmicToneMapping + toneMappingExposure=1.8 is standard photonicRealism renderer config (clm-pr-10)
MeshPhysicalMaterial visor config: transmission + clearcoat + iridescence confirmed API-compatible (clm-pr-04)
SkeletonUtils.clone() is the correct pattern for multiple independent animated spacekid instances (clm-pr-07)
OrbitControls disable/enable on dragstart/dragend is required for coexistence with DragControls (clm-pr-17)
Kenney Space Kit: 150 CC0 GLTF/GLB assets for SpaceshipEntity — no animation clips expected (ships/planets) (clm-pr-19)
Kenney Animated Characters: GLTF format confirmed by donmccurdy/Three.js core team; specific itch.io pack available (partial g-pr-01)
gapsConfirmed: g-pr-01 PARTIAL: Kenney Animated Characters 3 AnimationMixer compat unverified — requires runtime GLB download test
g-pr-03: MeshSSSNodeMaterial Chromebook performance not benchmarked — requires device profiling
snap grid + DragControls drag event integration: Math.round formula confirmed but not tested against InteractionMode board positions

prototypes.label: Procedural Geometry Stack (H-2) + miniplex ECS (H-4) + Gesture+Spatial (H-6)
prototypes.researchMethod: Reference implementation curation from official docs + authoritative tutorials

criticalGapsResolved.description: Procedural spacekid visual appeal for educational engagement
criticalGapsResolved.resolution: UNRESOLVABLE by reference research alone — requires informal usability test. Technical finding: MeshPhysicalMaterial + IBL on SphereGeometry (helmet) + CylinderGeometry (body) produces photonicRealism aesthetic. Multi-part primitive character is standard in many educational games (Minecraft-style). Engagement data for Korean elementary school students not available.
criticalGapsResolved.remainingGap: Pedagogical expressiveness requirement unresolved — must be determined by user study or stakeholder decision. Recommend: build procedural SpacekidEntity first (fast), evaluate engagement, pivot to H-1 GLTF if insufficient.

researchFindings.topic: Procedural SpaceshipEntity geometry — Three.js primitive assembly
researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: // Spaceship from primitives: const ship = new THREE.Group(); const hull = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.5, 1.0, 3.0, 12), hullMaterial); const wing = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.BoxGeometry(3.0, 0.1, 1.0), wingMaterial); wing.position.set(0, 0, 0); const cockpit = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.6, 12, 8), visorMaterial); cockpit.position.set(0, 1.2, 0); ship.add(hull, wing, cockpit);
researchFindings.integrationPoint: Replaces create-resonance-loop.ts. ProceduralSpaceshipSpec DATA entity drives geometry parameters. Compatible with H-3 ComponentFactory.
researchFindings.notes: BufferGeometryUtils.mergeGeometries() optional for static parts — reduces draw calls. Dynamic/animated parts must remain separate. import { mergeGeometries } from 'three/addons/utils/BufferGeometryUtils.js'. Existing mathcrew uses same primitive approach (CylinderGeometry + TorusGeometry + CylinderGeometry) — H-2 extends existing pattern.

researchFindings.topic: Procedural SpacekidEntity geometry — character from primitives
researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: // Spacekid from primitives: const spacekid = new THREE.Group(); const helmet = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.3, 16, 12), helmetMaterial); const body = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.2, 0.25, 0.6, 10), suitMaterial); const visor = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.22, 12, 8), visorMaterial); visor.scale.set(0.9, 0.7, 0.6); visor.position.set(0, 0, 0.12); helmet.add(visor); body.position.set(0, -0.45, 0); spacekid.add(helmet, body);
researchFindings.integrationPoint: ProceduralCharacterSpec DATA entity drives geometry params (helmetRadius, suitColor, visorTransmission). Replaces create-pulse-shard.ts.
researchFindings.notes: Visor uses MeshPhysicalMaterial transmission=0.9 + iridescence=0.3. Helmet uses MeshPhysicalMaterial metalness=0.1 + roughness=0.4. This matches existing mathcrew holographic/crystal aesthetic extended with IBL. CRITICAL RISK: expressive character interaction (arm gesture, facial expression) NOT achievable with static primitive geometry.

researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: // In animation render loop: const t = clock.getElapsedTime(); // Idle bob: spacekidGroup.position.y = Math.sin(t * 1.5) * 0.05; // Celebration spin: if (state === 'celebrate') spacekidGroup.rotation.y += 0.05; // Walk cycle (procedural): leftLeg.rotation.x = Math.sin(t * 3) * 0.3; rightLeg.rotation.x = Math.sin(t * 3 + Math.PI) * 0.3;
researchFindings.integrationPoint: CharacterAnimationSystem LOGIC — runs in game-loop render frame. Replaces AnimationMixer per character. Much simpler than skeletal system.
researchFindings.notes: Achievable animations: idle float/bob, celebrate spin, error shake, walk cycle (if legs exist as separate meshes). NOT achievable: boarding animation, nuanced facial expression, hand gestures. Design constraint: math interactions that require boarding/disembarking (req-pr-08) need more complex procedural or GLTF.

researchFindings.topic: InstancedMesh constraint — INCOMPATIBLE with SkinnedMesh (H-2 design constraint)
researchFindings.keyAPIPattern: // For STATIC procedural entities (SpaceshipEntity parts): const instancedHull = new THREE.InstancedMesh(hullGeometry, hullMaterial, count); instancedHull.setMatrixAt(i, matrix); instancedHull.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true; // For ANIMATED spacekid: DO NOT use InstancedMesh. Use separate Mesh per character: const spacekid1 = spacekidGroup.clone(); const spacekid2 = spacekidGroup.clone();
researchFindings.integrationPoint: CRITICAL DESIGN CONSTRAINT for H-2 — SpaceshipEntity static parts can use InstancedMesh. SpacekidEntity procedural meshes use Group.clone() — NOT InstancedMesh. Affects entity budget planning.
researchFindings.notes: InstancedMesh INCOMPATIBLE with SkinnedMesh/skeletal animation. For H-2 procedural spacekid <50 instances: Group.clone() is trivial. For H-4 miniplex ECS: entity remove handler must call geometry.dispose() + material.dispose() manually — miniplex.world.remove() does NOT auto-dispose Three.js resources.

researchFindings.topic: miniplex ECS (H-4) — World API + Three.js entity management
researchFindings.integrationPoint: Replaces Factory+Registry Map<> in H-2+H-4+H-6 stack. miniplex World becomes authoritative entity registry. Systems (animationSystem, renderSystem, interactionSystem) are plain functions querying world.with().
researchFindings.notes: miniplex v2.0.0 — last published 3 years ago, 2,587 weekly downloads. MIT license. Bundle size: ~2KB gzipped (see bundlephobia). Key constraint: world.remove() does NOT dispose Three.js geometry/material — must subscribe to onEntityRemoved for manual cleanup. API: world.add(entity), world.remove(entity), world.with(...components) for queries, world.addComponent(entity, key, value) — direct property mutation bypasses indexing. Entities are plain JS objects — idiomatic TypeScript. No built-in scheduling — fits mathcrew requestAnimationFrame loop. Comparison with H-3: miniplex adds external dependency (~2KB) but provides reactive queries (onEntityAdded/Removed) and cleaner cross-system entity sharing vs Map<>.

researchFindings.topic: Gesture+Spatial Interaction Paradigm (H-6) — pointer event velocity classification
researchFindings.integrationPoint: GestureEventDispatch ACTION — replaces DragControls proxy pattern. Requires custom implementation (no Three.js built-in). PlacementZone DATA replaces SnapGridConfig DATA. InteractionMode transition fires on 'tap' or gesture completion.
researchFindings.notes: H-6 key risks vs H-5: (1) No Three.js built-in — requires bespoke GestureClassifier implementation. (2) OS edge gesture conflicts (swipe-up = home/dock) are higher risk without DragControls boundary controls. (3) Flick velocity threshold (600 px/sec) is heuristic — requires device calibration. (4) PlacementZone approach rejects discrete grid snap — changes math manipulative placement semantics (risk for structured math games like mathcrew). Recommended libraries if H-6 pursued: Hammer.js (MIT, tap/swipe/pinch/rotate) or ZingTouch (velocity-based swipe sensitivity tuning). H-5 (DragControls+Proxy) is lower risk for mathcrew's structured grid-based math interactions.

mathcrewFiles.create-pulse-shard.ts: REPLACE with create-spacekid-entity.ts — ProceduralCharacterSpec + primitive geometry assembly + ProceduralRigAnimator. No GLTF dependency. In H-4 stack: world.add(entity) registers to miniplex World.
mathcrewFiles.create-resonance-loop.ts: REPLACE with create-spaceship-entity.ts — ProceduralSpaceshipSpec + CylinderGeometry + BoxGeometry + TorusGeometry assembly. InstancedMesh for identical static spaceship parts.
mathcrewFiles.materials.ts: EXTEND with photonicMaterialPresets same as H-1. suitBody + visor + hullMaterial as MeshPhysicalMaterial configs.
mathcrewFiles.interaction-controller.ts: H-6: REPLACE DragControls with GestureClassifier — PointerEvents + velocity analysis. PlacementZone replaces SnapGridConfig. OR H-5 proxy pattern applies if H-6 gesture risk rejected.
mathcrewFiles.teaching-story-framework.ts: METAPHOR LAYER ONLY — identical to H-1 migration. No VT/CRA/beats changes.

newFiles: src/rendering/photonic-pipeline.ts — PMREMGenerator + RGBELoader setup (identical to H-1)
src/scene/systems/gesture-classifier.ts — PointerEvents + velocity analysis → GestureType (H-6 only)
preservedContracts: 10 InteractionModes: registerMode() pattern preserved — gesture completion fires same InteractionMode transition
SpeechBubble CSS overlay via Vector3.project(): unchanged

estimatedMigrationWeeks.miniplex ECS integration: 1 week (World setup + query definitions + onEntityAdded hooks + manual disposal)

integrationAssessment.hybridRecommendation: SpaceshipEntity: H-2 procedural (extends existing mathcrew pattern). SpacekidEntity: start H-2 procedural (fast), pivot to H-1 GLTF if engagement insufficient. Component system: H-3 Factory+Registry preferred over H-4 miniplex for <50 entity scale — miniplex adds external dependency for marginal benefit at this scale. Interaction: H-5 DragControls+Proxy preferred over H-6 Gesture+Spatial — H-6 OS edge gesture conflicts and bespoke implementation cost are high risk for structured math game grid placement. H-6 is a viable enhancement AFTER H-5 is validated for open-ended exploration modes.

claimsValidated: BufferGeometryUtils.mergeGeometries() for static spaceship parts — reduces draw calls, import from 'three/addons/utils/BufferGeometryUtils.js'
InstancedMesh INCOMPATIBLE with SkinnedMesh — procedural characters use Group.clone() (clm-pr-23 CONFIRMED)
PMREMGenerator + scene.environment setup: shared between H-1 and H-2 stacks (clm-pr-11)
miniplex v2.0.0 API: world.with('spacekid', 'animationMixer') returns typed iterable — TypeScript-safe, zero-overhead for...of iteration [Official]
miniplex onEntityAdded/onEntityRemoved: enables reactive Three.js resource allocation + cleanup — critical pattern for animated entity lifecycle
miniplex world.remove() does NOT auto-dispose Three.js geometry/material — manual disposal via onEntityRemoved subscription required [Official]
H-6 PointerEvents: setPointerCapture(pointerId) + releasePointerCapture for reliable cross-device gesture tracking (MDN best practice)
H-6 flick velocity threshold: ~600 px/sec distinguishes drag from flick in educational game context [Synthesis]
ProceduralRigAnimator sin/cos time animation: achievable for idle bob/float/spin — NOT for complex boarding sequence
gapsConfirmed: g-pr-02: Procedural spacekid pedagogical engagement for Korean elementary students — UNRESOLVABLE by research alone, requires user study
g-pr-05: ProceduralRigAnimator boarding animation expressiveness — complex sequence not achievable with simple sin/cos; req-pr-08 boarding interaction requires GLTF fallback or state machine
H-4 miniplex: query performance at <50 entities vs Factory+Registry Map<> — both are O(n) trivial, no benchmark needed at this scale but miniplex adds ~2KB bundle
H-6 gesture: OS edge gesture conflicts (iOS swipe-up dock, Android nav gestures) — requires device-specific pointer capture tuning not resolvable by research alone

failureModes.description: MeshSSSNodeMaterial FPS on Chromebook — canonical g-pr-01. Reference research confirms MeshPhysicalMaterial IBL mechanism. Cannot substitute for device benchmark: per-fragment SSS cost on Intel Iris Xe / UHD 620 with n8ao SSAO active is unknown. SSS is selective/optional and does not block core H-1, but must be validated before committing to SpacekidEntity face material spec.
failureModes.affectedClaim: MeshSSSNodeMaterial selective use on spacekid face achieves photonicRealism within Chromebook frame budget

nextExperiment.method: stats.js FPS benchmark — render scene with 1 SpacekidEntity using MeshSSSNodeMaterial on head mesh vs baseline MeshPhysicalMaterial (metalness=0.1, roughness=0.4). n8ao SSAO active. Measure FPS over 5s.
nextExperiment.passCriterion: > 50fps on Chromebook-class GPU (Intel Iris Xe / UHD 620)
nextExperiment.failAction: Substitute MeshPhysicalMaterial with roughness/specular approximation. Disable SSS. SSS becomes opt-in feature flag behind performance tier detection.

failureModes.description: SkeletonUtils.clone() x5 draw call budget — canonical g-pr-02. Reference research confirms SkeletonUtils.clone() produces independent bone hierarchies (clm-pr-07, [Synthesis] 0.90) and estimates ~10-25 instances at 60fps. Cannot substitute for runtime measurement: renderer.info.render.calls with 5 simultaneous SkinnedMesh instances + all AnimationMixers active on target hardware is unverified.
failureModes.affectedClaim: 5+ simultaneous SpacekidEntity instances maintain < 100 draw calls per PerformanceBudgetPolicy (obj-pr-13) at 60fps

nextExperiment.method: Load SpacekidEntity GLTF, SkeletonUtils.clone() x5, attach AnimationMixer per clone, play idle animation on all. Read renderer.info.render.calls after one render frame. Measure FPS over 5s on Chromebook-class GPU.
nextExperiment.passCriterion: < 100 total draw calls per frame (PerformanceBudgetPolicy obj-pr-13). > 50fps sustained.
nextExperiment.failAction: Reduce max simultaneous SpacekidEntity instances. Consider geometry merging for static mesh parts. Evaluate LOD (detail reduction at distance) for instances beyond 3.

failureModes.description: DragControls proxy + AnimationMixer concurrent jitter — canonical g-pr-03. Reference research (rf-T7-05, [Official+Tutorial] 0.95) confirms proxy mechanism: invisible BoxGeometry proxy bound to DragControls, modelGroup.position.copy(proxy.position) per frame, AnimationMixer.update() continues uninterrupted. g-pr-04 RESOLVED for position sync. The specific case of crossFadeTo() triggered during an active drag (0.3s blend transition while root position is being overwritten by proxy copy each frame) is untested — potential for root motion jitter if any clip has root motion baked.
failureModes.affectedClaim: crossFadeTo() during active DragControls drag produces no visible root position jitter in 0.3s transition window

nextExperiment.method: Load animated SpacekidEntity GLTF. Start idle AnimationAction. Initiate DragControls drag. During drag, trigger crossFadeTo(walkAction, 0.3, true). Observe root Group position for jitter over 0.3s blend window. Check if any clip has root motion (rootBone.position keyframes).
nextExperiment.passCriterion: No visible position jitter during 0.3s crossFade transition. Root position remains locked to proxy.position each frame.
nextExperiment.failAction: If jitter detected: check GLB for root motion bake. If root motion present, bake in-place in Blender (Object → Apply → Visual Transform). If no root motion, jitter is a frame-order issue — move modelGroup.position.copy() to postRender step after mixer.update().

failureModes.description: Kenney Animated Characters 3 GLTF + r183 AnimationMixer compatibility unverified. Kenney Space Kit (ships/planets) confirmed CC0 GLB. Mixamo FBX→Blender→GLB is the confirmed fallback. Does not block H-1 architecture — only the specific Kenney character asset source choice.
failureModes.affectedClaim: SpacekidEntity source: Kenney Animated Characters 3 GLTF format compatible with r183 AnimationMixer
failureModes.recommendation: Download kenney-assets.itch.io/animated-characters-3. Load with GLTFLoader in r183. Verify gltf.animations[] non-empty. Test mixer.clipAction().play(). If failed, proceed with Mixamo FBX→Blender→GLB as primary pipeline.

failureModes.description: Snap grid formula (Math.round(pos / CELL_SIZE) * CELL_SIZE) confirmed correct in isolation. CELL_SIZE alignment with mathcrew's existing spatial grid coordinate system unverified. Low risk — formula is correct; integration requires reading interaction-controller.ts coordinate units.
failureModes.affectedClaim: DragControls snap grid CELL_SIZE aligns with InteractionMode board positions in existing mathcrew coordinate system
failureModes.recommendation: Read interaction-controller.ts and array-builder/resonance-trial-sequencer modes to determine spatial coordinate system. Map CELL_SIZE to existing grid unit. Add snap integration test against registerMode() callback.

failureModes.description: Procedural geometry SpacekidEntity (SphereGeometry helmet + CylinderGeometry body) visual appeal for Korean elementary school students not determinable by reference research alone. Technical implementation confirmed valid. Engagement sufficiency requires user study or stakeholder decision.
failureModes.affectedClaim: Procedural SpacekidEntity achieves sufficient pedagogical engagement vs GLTF character (H-1)
failureModes.recommendation: Build procedural SpacekidEntity first (1 week vs 2-3 weeks GLTF). Conduct informal engagement test with 3-5 target students. H-3 Factory+Registry ensures pivot to H-1 GLTF is a single registry.set() swap.

failureModes.description: req-pr-08 (boarding animation) requires multi-phase procedural animation not achievable with sin/cos ProceduralRigAnimator. State machine + IK-approximated poses needed for believable boarding.
failureModes.affectedClaim: ProceduralRigAnimator sufficient for all req-pr-08 animations including boarding/disembarking
failureModes.recommendation: Descope boarding from H-2. Implement as: tween Group.position to spaceship interior + scale-to-zero + scale-from-zero on disembark. Full boarding animation is p1 enhancement only achievable with H-1 GLTF + AnimationMixer.

evaluatorGate.summary: 14 PASS, 0 FAIL, 1 N/A. Session mathcrew-photonic-realism-001. Independent verification: bun test 33/33 (T7, 18ms) + 35/35 (T8, 19ms), bunx tsc --noEmit 0 errors. No debate triggered.

ruleDetails.R12: PASS — All 43 objects have DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS. 6 hypotheses scored. DevCon Phase 2->3 documented.

evaluatorGate.debateReason: Pass rate delta=0% (both 1.0). No D/L/A misclassification. No new failure types. H-4 deprioritization evidence-based. Conditions not met.
evaluatorCaveats: Prototypes are research-complete (reference implementation curation), not build artifacts
3 critical gaps require runtime benchmarks: g-pr-01 (SSS FPS), g-pr-02 (clone draw calls), g-pr-03 (DragControls jitter)
FM-T8-001 (high): H-2 procedural engagement unresolvable by research — requires user study
H-2 worst scenario evidenceSufficiency=insufficient — recommendation is hybrid H-2->H-1 path
All propagation paths partial — designed but not implemented
Teaching framework migration METAPHOR-LAYER-ONLY — VT/CRA/beats/ts-fsrs contracts preserved
